description:
The map displays the geographic distribution of Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR) "representative tracks" across Southern and Eastern Europe, including the Balkan Peninsula and parts of the Italian Peninsula. Labelled countries include SLOVENIA, CROATIA, BOSNIA AND HERZEGOVINA, SERBIA, ROMANIA, BULGARIA, ALBANIA, and GREECE; ITALY is partially visible on the western edge. Major seas identified are the Adriatic Sea, Tyrrhenian Sea, Ionian Sea, Sea of Crete, and Mediterranean Sea.

Grey circles with black outlines are distributed across the landmasses and seas, indicating the locations of these InSAR representative tracks. The varying sizes of the circles may denote differences in measurement point density, although no explicit legend for size is provided. High concentrations of tracks are visible across Greece, particularly its southern and eastern regions, and along the coastlines of Albania and Croatia. Additional tracks are located west of Italy and two tracks are shown in Romania.

A prominent red line, potentially representing a geological or tectonic boundary, traverses the map, originating west of the Italian Peninsula, passing through the Ionian Sea, south of Greece, and extending into the Mediterranean Sea. The distribution of InSAR tracks frequently aligns with or is in close proximity to this red boundary line. No scale bar, compass rose, or detailed legend is included on the map.
